creator: laszlo-rdr-keethu9thi@karolyi.hu
text:
(In reply to Bob Smith from comment #92)
With FreeBSD 13.0 out, and it having changed its src and ports repository to git (https://docs.freebsd.org/en/books/handbook/mirrors/#git), the process changes to:

1. install git
2. git clone -o freebsd -b releng/$(uname -r | cut -d'-' -f1,1) https://git.FreeBSD.org/src.git /usr/src
3. git clone -o freebsd https://git.freebsd.org/ports.git /usr/ports
4. cd /usr/ports/net/realtek-re-kmod/
5. make install
6. echo 'if_re_load="YES"' >> /boot/loader.conf
7. echo 'if_re_name="/boot/modules/if_re.ko"' >> /boot/loader.conf

Just thought I'd update this since SVN is no longer the default way to check out sources and ports.
